01-adopted::FR-01.01.title: Landing page. Lists every registered task across all projects as cards grouped by state (active / idle / completed / failed). Includes a sidebar split-button to create a new Pipeline / Iterate / Task or to Continue a pipeline. Each card shows a state icon, an actions menu, and a launch CTA; clicking a card navigates to its TaskDetail. Within each column — and in the alternative List view — tasks default to **Last-Modified-descending** order (newest activity on top; source precedence `lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs` → `launchedAt` → `createdAt`, ties broken deterministically by `taskId`; shared helper `client/src/lib/taskSort.ts`). The List view additionally exposes Title / Updated column-header sort toggles (asc↔desc). Master TaskCards (one per Run when a project's `shipwright_run_config.json` reports `runs[]`) render their child phase-tasks inline with conditional banners for failed / needs_validation / complete / stale states. **Iterate-2026-07-09-w3 (campaign webui-pipeline-convergence W3) MODIFY:** the Pipelines lane is a `PipelineLaneCard` host that picks the Run's representation by `resolveRunMode(config)` — a `single_session` Run renders as a **campaign-like `SingleSessionRunCard`** (a steady high-water 7-phase progress bar over `config.pipeline` + the real `phase_tasks` checklist + ONE state-aware **Launch → Resume** CTA `MasterRunLaunchButton` that launches the `/shipwright-run` master via `useLaunchMasterRun`; the CTA hides once the Run is terminal), NOT the per-phase-Continue `MasterTaskCard`; `multi_session` / mode-less-legacy Runs keep the UNCHANGED `MasterTaskCard`. The per-phase **Continue Pipeline** create-menu entry is hidden for a single-session Run (the master drives every phase). Server refuses a second concurrent master with `409 master_run_already_attached`. **Iterate-2026-07-10 (FR-01.45) MODIFY:** when the single-session run is paused at the design gate (`useDesignGate(...).active`), the `SingleSessionRunCard` additionally renders a `DesignGatePanel` (Review-mockups affordance) above the Launch/Resume CTA.

01-adopted::FR-01.02.title: Three-pane layout: left FolderTree of the project root (gitignore-aware, lazy-expanded), center BubbleTranscript rendering the JSONL as chat-style messages with markdown + code + ANSI-stripped tool output, right SmartViewer for selected files (Markdown / Code / Text / Image / Video / Mermaid renderers). Pane widths and collapsed state persist in localStorage. The header carries an editable title (renames take effect on the next launch via Claude's `--name` flag) and a state-dependent CTA (launch / resume / fork). No chat composer — that is a load-bearing absence per ADR-034.

01-adopted::FR-01.03.title: CRUD for the project registry persisted at `~/.shipwright-webui/projects.json`. The 4-step wizard collects name, path, stack profile (auto-suggested from the resolved profiles dir), and a color tag. Stack profile selection drives downstream capability gates (Preview button, dev-server command).

01-adopted::FR-01.04.title: Best-effort surface for pending Claude tool_use blocks (notably AskUserQuestion) across every tracked task. Derived from JSONL by walking unmatched tool_use ids. Each row links back to its TaskDetail and supports a one-shot dismiss to silence the reminder. **Iterate-2026-05-15 (inbox-awaiting-user):** the Inbox additionally surfaces plain-text "how should I proceed?" questions — when a session's latest turn is a turn-ended assistant message (no tool_use, no later user reply) whose text ends with `?` or contains an enumerated option list, it appears as a `kind: "text_question"` row. These rows auto-clear when the user replies in the terminal and carry no dismiss action (FR-01.14 stays AUQ-only).

01-adopted::FR-01.05.title: Read-only view of Claude CLI version, the resolved profiles directory, the launcher's three-shell output samples, and a list of currently observed sessions. Fronted by a banner when the detected CLI is below `MIN_SUPPORTED_CLI` (see `core/cli-compat.ts`).

01-adopted::FR-01.06.title: Minimal placeholder page. Most settings now live inside the user's Claude client (model, autonomy, plugin dirs); the WebUI keeps only its own UI preferences. Persisted at `~/.shipwright-webui/settings.json` with `proper-lockfile`-guarded writes.

01-adopted::FR-01.07.title: Liveness probe used by `dev_server.py`, smoke tests, and the install-windows autostart. Returns `{status, version, uptime}`.

01-adopted::FR-01.08.title: GET returns every persisted task from `sdk-sessions.json` with derived state (active / idle / completed / failed) and timestamps. POST creates a new task with a pre-bound `crypto.randomUUID()` session-id, optional projectId / phaseTaskRef, and the title that flows into Claude via `--name` at launch. **Iterate-2026-05-14 (ADR-100 — lead-foundation-task-schema, leadwright Phase 1):** POST body additively accepts five leadwright-routing fields (`domain`, `priority`, `complexityHint`, `tags`, `blockedBy`) — all optional, persisted verbatim on the `ExternalTask` record so the leadwright daemon (separate repo) can route/claim. `schemaVersion` stays at 3; legacy POST bodies without these fields succeed unchanged. **Iterate-2026-05-14 (ADR-101 — triage-tab):** `SdkSessionsStore.create()` now also accepts an optional `promotedFromTriageId: string` argument (back-ref to `<project>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl`) populated by the new POST `/api/triage/:projectId/promote` route (FR-01.30). The store gains a `findByPromotedFromTriageId(triageId)` finder (mirrors `findByPhaseTaskId`) so promote retries are idempotent. POST `/api/external/tasks` itself does NOT accept `promotedFromTriageId` — only the triage promote route does (narrow write surface, mirrors the daemon-only-fields pattern from ADR-100 MED-4).

01-adopted::FR-01.09.title: GET returns the full task row with derived state. PATCH allows title / projectId / autonomy edits; ELOCKED bubbles up as 409 so the client can retry. DELETE asks the user to confirm in non-terminal states.

01-adopted::FR-01.10.title: Returns the three-shell copy-command (PowerShell / cmd.exe / POSIX) for the task. The launcher routes via `substitutePlaceholders` with full action context (`{cd.prefix}`, `{project.path}`, `{task.uuid}`, optional slash command). Supports two mutually exclusive intents: a freeform `actionId` from `.shipwright-webui/actions.json` / `default-actions.json`, or a `phaseTaskRef.phaseTaskId` that re-reads `shipwright_run_config.json` server-side and rejects mismatched session-uuids (409 phase_task_session_uuid_mismatch). Mixing both yields 400 mixed_launch_intents. **Iterate-2026-05-04 (ADR-068-A1):** the response body shape is unchanged, but the primary client consumer is now the auto-execute flow — `LaunchCoordinatorContext.dispatchAutoLaunch` queues `commands[shellKind] + "\r"` for delivery via `socket.send({type:"data", payload})` once the embedded-terminal WS reaches `ready=true && role="writer"` AND the prompt-readiness handshake (250ms quiesce after first onData burst, 3s hard cap) clears. Clipboard write is removed from the primary path. Plan-D'' compliance via amended "user-initiated" interpretation: an explicit click on `Launch` / `Resume` / `Relaunch` CTA is sufficient — equivalent to the previous Strg+V+Enter ritual; pty-manager whitelist remains the architectural enforcement line.

01-adopted::FR-01.11.title: Same shape as launch but for `--resume` of an existing session into a new task row. Plugin dirs are re-passed because `--plugin-dir` does not survive `--resume` reliably.

01-adopted::FR-01.12.title: Reads `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<uuid>.jsonl` as a UTF-8-safe byte range. Client passes `?fromByte=<n>&expectFingerprint=<fp>` so the server stays stateless across tabs. Cuts are made on `\n` boundaries only. EBUSY/EPERM/EACCES/ENOENT retried up to 6 attempts with 50→1600 ms backoff.

01-adopted::FR-01.13.title: Walks every tracked task's JSONL via `inbox-derive.ts` and returns unmatched tool_use blocks (best-effort). Memoized by `(sessionUuid, mtimeMs, dismissedKey, contentLength)` so the route stays sub-100 ms across hundreds of sessions. **Iterate-2026-05-15 (inbox-awaiting-user):** in addition to unmatched tool_use blocks, `inbox-derive.ts` runs `detectAwaitingUserQuestion()` — a content-only walk that flags a session whose latest turn is a turn-ended assistant message asking the user a question (text ends with `?` or holds an enumerated option list). Response items gain a `kind: "ask_tool" \

01-adopted::FR-01.14.title: Adds the toolUseId to the dismissed set so subsequent inbox derivations skip it. Local-only; the JSONL is never mutated.

01-adopted::FR-01.15.title: Marks the task as completed in the registry without spawning anything in the user's terminal. Pure metadata flip; the JSONL stays untouched.

01-adopted::FR-01.16.title: Resolves the merged action catalog for a project: `<project>/.shipwright-webui/actions.json` overlaying `default-actions.json`. mtime-cached on the server. The response feeds the New-* modal and the launch button menus. **Iterate-2026-05-14 (ADR-NNN — lead-foundation-task-schema):** the bundled `new-task` and `new-iterate` actions' `modal_fields` arrays gain five leadwright-routing entries (`domain`, `priority`, `complexityHint`, `tags`, `blockedBy`); the server-side `SUPPORTED_MODAL_FIELDS` allowlist (in `actions-schema-validator.ts`) is extended in lockstep so projects with custom `.shipwright-webui/actions.json` can opt in.

01-adopted::FR-01.17.title: Spawns the project's `dev_server.command` (from its stack profile) with `shell: false` for command-injection safety. Lifecycle owned by `core/preview-session-manager.ts`; killed on server shutdown. Visibility is gated by `stack.frontend` AND `dev_server.command` AND `actions.preview.enabled`.

01-adopted::FR-01.18.title: Read-only forwarder for `<project.path>/shipwright_run_config.json`. Per-row fault isolation, torn-read retry, 5 s last-good cache. WebUI never mutates this file (DO-NOT regression guard).

01-adopted::FR-01.19.title: Lazy-expand listing for the FolderTree component. Honors gitignore (mtime-cached rules), uses path-traversal-safe realpath comparison (NOT startsWith), and rejects null-byte input.

01-adopted::FR-01.20.title: Reads a single file under the project root with the same realpath-based path guard. Used by the SmartViewer right pane.

01-adopted::FR-01.21.title: One-shot: writes an empty (but schema-valid) `.shipwright-webui/actions.json` under the project root if and only if the file does not already exist. Used by the wizard / settings to scaffold customization.

01-adopted::FR-01.22.title: Returns CLI version (refreshed on demand), profiles dir, sample copy-commands, observed-session count, and a `min_supported_cli` warning flag. Powers the `/diagnostics` page and the bootstrap CLI banner.

01-adopted::FR-01.23.title: Lists every stack profile from the resolved profiles dir (override → monorepo → bundled). Used by the wizard to populate the profile dropdown.

01-adopted::FR-01.24.title: GET lists all registered projects. POST creates a new one (validates the path, captures the chosen profile and color). Persisted via `proper-lockfile` to `~/.shipwright-webui/projects.json`. POST also generates a portable VS Code workspace file at `<project.path>/.shipwright-webui/<slug>.code-workspace` (idempotent, atomic temp+rename) so the project opens in VS Code with one double-click — see ADR-059.

01-adopted::FR-01.25.title: GET returns the project row. PATCH updates name / profile / color. **DELETE (iterate-2026-07-06-project-delete-cascades-tasks):** removes the project row AND **cascade-removes every task whose `projectId` points at it** (store rows + best-effort scrollback + snapshot side-files, mirroring the single-task DELETE cascade) so no orphaned synthetic "Unassigned" bucket is left behind; the response is `{ ok: true, deletedTaskCount }`. On-disk Claude JSONL under `~/.claude` is NOT touched (webui is a read-only observer). Prior behavior left the tasks with a dangling `projectId`, which perpetually synthesized a phantom, un-clearable Unassigned row at runtime (`SdkSessionsStore` O26 only self-healed to "unassigned" on the next boot).

01-adopted::FR-01.27.title: Settings page lets the user pick a registered project, see its current actions-source state (Custom / Bundled / Malformed), and either upload a JSON file (POST → server runs JSON.parse + `validateActionsSchema` + `checkContractVersion`, atomic write under realpath path-guard) or reset to bundled (DELETE removes the user file). Size-capped (≤256 KB), JSON content-type only. Re-uses the FR-01.21 directory-creation precedent.

01-adopted::FR-01.28.title: TaskDetail center pane renders a Toggle-Tab `Transcript / Terminal`; when `Terminal` is active, an xterm.js panel (lazy-loaded) connects to `GET /api/terminal/:taskId/ws` via `@hono/node-ws`. The WS upgrade is the AUTHORITATIVE pty creation path (ensure-or-create); `POST /api/terminal/:taskId/spawn` is an idempotent prewarm; `POST /api/terminal/:taskId/close` kills the pty (scrollback retained). PtyManager whitelists shell binaries only (`pwsh / powershell / cmd / bash / zsh / sh / fish`, basename-normalised) and refuses `claude` as spawn target. First-attach is writer; subsequent attaches are read-only with a banner. PTY auto-killed when last connection detaches; 30-min idle ceiling forces kill regardless. Outbound applies drop-while-saturated backpressure on `ws.bufferedAmount`. **Iterate-2026-05-04 (ADR-068-A1) extension:** (a) launch CTA dispatches `LaunchCoordinatorContext.dispatchAutoLaunch(commands, resume)` (NOT `webui:launch-copied`); pendingLaunch token-deduped; cancelled on reader-role / page-unmount / 30s timeout. (b) `pty.onData(data)` synchronously appends to `<registryDir>/terminal-scrollback/<taskId>.log` via `ScrollbackStore` (4-state rotation state-machine: NORMAL → ROTATING → ROTATION_FLUSH → NORMAL; `.log` → `.log.1` Windows-safe rename retry; UUID validation + realpath-at-op-time path-guard). (c) WS upgrade `onOpen` calls `pty.pause()`, replays scrollback as chunked `replay_start` / `replay_chunk` (64 KiB) / `replay_separator` / `replay_end` envelopes, flushes liveBuffer, calls `pty.resume()`. (d) NEW `POST /api/terminal/:taskId/clear-scrollback` (loud throws on failure). (e) Boot + daily periodic TTL sweep (default 1 day, `SHIPWRIGHT_TERMINAL_SCROLLBACK_TTL_DAYS`); active-task-aware skip; bounded 100/pass; oldest-first. (f) `MAX_BYTES=0` disables persistence. (g) Privacy disclosure UI surfaces retention period + Windows-permission-limitation note + "may contain secrets" warning. Tab choice persisted in `localStorage["webui:embedded-terminal-default-tab"]` (default `"terminal"`). **Iterate-2026-05-18 (terminal-copy-paste):** keyboard text copy (`Ctrl+C` on selection / `Ctrl+Insert`) + paste (`Ctrl+V` / `Shift+Insert`) via a single `attachCustomKeyEventHandler`; copy uses the `execCommand` fallback so it works over Tailscale http; a non-secure-context paste shows an inline "use right-click → Paste" hint instead of failing silently. **Iterate-2026-07-07 (terminal-osc52-clipboard) MODIFY:** terminal-text COPY is now Claude Code's own **OSC 52** relay, not WebUI `Ctrl+C` interception — `EmbeddedTerminal` registers `term.parser.registerOscHandler(52, …)` (`terminal-osc52.ts`) which decodes the payload and writes via `copyText` (execCommand fallback, http-safe); READ requests (`52;c;?`) are DENIED (never leak the OS clipboard to a program), oversized/malformed/empty payloads are swallowed. The WebUI no longer intercepts `Ctrl+C`/`Ctrl+Insert` (they pass through as interrupt/SIGINT); copy-on-selection + its Settings toggle, the redraw-proof selection cache + Copy pill (iterate-2026-07-06), and the "Maus-Modus aktiv" mouse-mode hint were removed. Paste (`Ctrl+V`/`Shift+Insert`/right-click) unchanged. **Iterate-2026-06-20 (mobile-terminal-touch-ux) MODIFY:** touch-scroll routes buffer-first — the normal buffer pans the real scrollback even when mouse-tracking is on, so a finger-pan scrolls at Claude's `--resume` "load full session / summary" picker (the alt-screen still forwards the wheel); the post-layout-change WebGL repaint is data-driven (`repaint-on-settle.ts` — repaint on each `term.onWriteParsed` until the async redraw settles, quiet gap or 3s cap), superseding the fixed 130/350ms trailing repaint that closed before the slow mobile redraw landed (fixes input-area smear on Transcript→Terminal switch / return-from-home); the touch `TerminalKeyBar` gains a white border + white glyph for legibility.

01-adopted::FR-01.29.title: DOM `paste` listener (capture phase) on the xterm container implements image-wins precedence: if `ClipboardData.items` contains any `image/*`, `preventDefault` + `POST /api/terminal/:taskId/paste-image` (multipart `image: File`); else if a text item is present, `socket.send({type:"data", payload})`; else no-op. Server validates magic-byte mime (PNG/JPEG/WEBP/GIF), 8 MiB cap, 9 MiB Content-Length precheck (413 fast-fail), persists to `<task.cwd>/.claude-pastes/img-<unix-ms>-<8 hex>.<ext>`, prunes by parsed-filename-timestamp keep-last-N (default 20, configurable via `SHIPWRIGHT_CLAUDE_PASTES_KEEP_LAST`), then `pty.write`s the shell-quoted absolute path via `quotePathForShell`. When `<task.cwd>/.gitignore` exists but lacks `.claude-pastes/`, the response carries `gitignoreSuggestion: true`; the client surfaces a toast with an `Append` button calling `POST /api/terminal/:taskId/append-gitignore` (idempotent, realpath-guarded; 404 when `.gitignore` is missing). **Iterate-2026-05-18 (terminal-copy-paste AC-8):** the DOM `paste` listener's text branch routes through `term.paste()` (line-ending + bracketed-paste normalization) instead of a raw `socket.send`, so a multi-line prompt no longer submits on its first line; image-wins precedence unchanged.

01-adopted::FR-01.30.title: New top-level `/triage` route + sidebar entry surfacing `<project>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl` items (`status==triage`) aggregated across all non-synthesized projects from the existing webui registry. **Read path:** `GET /api/triage/:projectId` returns the resolved view (TS port of `triage.read_all_items`; tolerant — skips JSONDecodeError lines; resolves last-status-wins by file order); `GET /api/triage/counts` returns `{ counts: Record<projectId, number>, total }`. Both auto-refresh every 30 s while the tab is active (no FS watcher reuse — `SessionWatcher` is Claude-JSONL-specific). **Promote** (`POST /api/triage/:projectId/promote`, body `{ triageId, priority, domain, complexityHint?, tags }`): server-side cross-store transaction in order — (1) resolve project; (2) idempotency check via `findByPromotedFromTriageId(triageId)` — if a task already exists, skip step 4; (3) acquire `sdk-sessions.json` lock; (4) acquire `<project>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl` lock (proper-lockfile); (5) re-read items + confirm `status==triage` (else 409); (6) `store.create({ ..., promotedFromTriageId: triageId, tags: [...defaultTags, ...userTags] })` where `defaultTags = ["source:" + item.source, "severity:" + item.severity, "triage:" + triageId]`; (7) append `{event:"status",newStatus:"promoted",by:"webui",reason:"webuiPromote",promotedTaskId:"EXT:" + taskId}` to triage.jsonl via `appendFileSync` (line-atomic); (8) release locks → 201. Partial-promote (step 7 fails after step 6) returns 207 with the new `taskId`; retry hits step 2's idempotency gate, skips create, re-attempts the flip — same `taskId`, no orphans. **Dismiss** + **Snooze** (`POST /api/triage/:projectId/{dismiss,snooze}`, body `{ triageId, reason? }`): single-file write — acquire triage.jsonl lock, validate `status==triage`, append status event, release. **Sidebar:** `<TriageBadge count={total} />` (orange, distinct from Inbox red); `null` when 0; "99+" when >99. **Cross-process lock note:** Python producers use `_FileLock` (msvcrt/fcntl byte-locks); webui uses `proper-lockfile` (directory-based). The two primitives don't compose — documented as a Known Limitation in ADR-101. Mitigation: append-mode writes are line-atomic at OS level for sub-PIPE_BUF writes; the only Python tool that writes status events for arbitrary ids is the manual `triage_promote.py` CLI (operator-only collision risk); last-status-wins by file order resolves any race in operator-intent's favor.

01-adopted::FR-01.31.title: The dev servers default-bind loopback for safety; non-loopback access (LAN / Tailscale) is opt-in. The Hono backend honors `HONO_HOST`, the Vite client honors `VITE_HOST`, and `SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE` (`local` \

01-adopted::FR-01.32.title: `POST /api/external/tasks/:id/backlog` flips an In-Progress task's `state` back to `draft` so it returns to the Backlog column of the TaskBoard. Sibling of FR-01.15 (Close) — a pure registry-state metadata flip; the JSONL and `shipwright_run_config.json` stay untouched. Accepts the five In-Progress states (`awaiting_external_start`, `active`, `idle`, `jsonl_missing`, `launch_failed`); `409 backlog_invalid_state` for the terminal `done` state; idempotent `200` when the task is already `draft`; `404` for an unknown id; `ELOCKED` on persist surfaces as `409`. Every history field (`launchedAt`, `firstJsonlObservedAt`, `lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs`, `actionId`, `phase`, inbox) is preserved — "Backlog" is a re-organisation gesture, not a reset. The transcript-poll state machine treats `draft` as sticky (no transition out of it).

01-adopted::FR-01.33.title: A read-only **Campaigns lane** above the kanban on `TaskBoardPage`, sibling of the Pipelines lane, surfacing Shipwright iterate campaigns under `<project>/.shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/`. **Read path:** `GET /api/campaigns/:projectId` returns one entry per campaign `{slug, intent, branchStrategy, expandsTriage, steps[], done, total, nextPending}` resolved by merging `status.json` (authoritative per-step `status`/`commit`/`branch`, written by `campaign_init.py`/`campaign_progress.py`) with the `campaign.md` `## Sub-Iterates` table (ordering + titles); `status.json` is optional — status derives from the table when absent. Unknown/synthesized project → `404`; symlink-escape of the campaigns dir → `403`; missing/empty campaigns dir → `200 {campaigns:[]}`. A malformed/half-written campaign dir is skipped (logged), never failing the whole response. **UI:** each card shows slug + one-line intent + a `done/total` progress bar + the ordered steps (✓ complete / ▶ next-pending / ○ pending) + a **Copy launch (Bx)** button copying `/shipwright-iterate "<nextPending.specPath>"` (the board has no embedded terminal, so this is a clipboard affordance, not auto-inject — disabled when no launchable step). `useCampaigns` polls at 3 s; the lane is hidden when every campaign is complete or the dir is empty. **Read-only — no new write surface** (`campaign_init.py`/`campaign_progress.py` own all campaign-state writes); no Triage coupling (import-boundary tested).

01-adopted::FR-01.34.title: A SECOND Campaigns-lane action — **Launch autonomous** — that opens a TaskDetail terminal auto-running `/shipwright-iterate --campaign <slug> --autonomous` (unlike FR-01.33's per-step clipboard copy). Takes the real iterate-launch path: `createTask` → server-built command → `sessionStorage["webui:pending-auto-launch:<taskId>"]` handoff → `navigate('/tasks/:id')` → embedded terminal auto-executes (ADR-068-A1). **Server:** new launch-route campaign branch (`applyCampaignBranch`, precedence phaseTaskRef → campaign → action → legacy). A **body-only** `campaignSlug` (never persisted on the task) is (a) regex-validated `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,128}$` + no `..`, (b) existence-checked against the realpath-guarded campaigns dir, then (c) turned into the fixed command via `core/launcher.ts buildCopyCommands` — the command is built ENTIRELY server-side (the client never dictates it; Architecture rule 1 / regression guard #19). `campaignSlug` + `actionId`/`phaseTaskRef` → `400 mixed_launch_intents`; invalid slug → `400 invalid_campaign_slug`; no such campaign → `400 campaign_not_found`. **Guardrail:** autonomous runs every remaining pending sub-iterate with no per-step gate, so the action is behind a confirm dialog showing the command + a no-gate warning; a risky pending step (status `failed`/`escalated`, or the forward-compat `plan_first`/`risk` sub-iterate frontmatter marker surfaced as `CampaignStep.planFirst`) is listed and the confirm is disabled until an explicit "run unattended anyway" ack. Disabled when nothing is pending / no project. **No new persisted write surface** — `campaignSlug` rides the launch body only; resume after JSONL takes the normal `--resume` shape.

01-adopted::FR-01.35.title: The SmartViewer markdown pane gains an **Edit** button (markdown files only, beside Pop out) opening a TipTap (ProseMirror) rich-text editor in a modal; edits serialize back to Markdown and write via a NEW path-guarded `PUT /api/external/projects/:projectId/file` — the first project-file write surface. Guards: `.md`/`.markdown` extension allowlist (`415`), `pathGuard`+`realPathGuard` (`400`), `MARKDOWN_WRITE_MAX_BYTES` 2 MiB byte-accurate cap (`413`), target-must-exist (`404`). **Optimistic concurrency:** GET `/file` emits a strong content-hash `ETag:"sha256:<hex>"`; PUT requires it as `If-Match` (missing → `400 precondition_required`, stale → `409 fingerprint_mismatch`) so it never clobbers a file a Claude session edited in the embedded terminal. Atomic tmp+rename. **Lossy-round-trip safety nets:** a mandatory pre-save line diff + a non-blocking warn banner when the file has frontmatter / raw HTML / footnotes / tables / task-lists; on `409` the editor KEEPS the user's edits + offers reload. Modal lazy-loaded (TipTap code-split). Authz inherits the loopback + project-scope + path-guard posture (no per-request auth, same as GET `/file`). Phase 1 = SmartViewer only (FolderTree context menu + GFM tables/images deferred).

01-adopted::FR-01.36.title: Replaces FR-01.33's per-step **Copy launch (Cx)** clipboard affordance with a **Launch (Cx)** button that opens a TaskDetail terminal auto-running `/shipwright-iterate "<specPath>"` for the campaign's `nextPending` step — same `createTask` → server-built command → `sessionStorage` handoff → `navigate('/tasks/:id')` → embedded-terminal auto-execute path as FR-01.34, scoped to ONE step (preserves the stacked dependency order). Direct one-click launch for an ordinary step; a Radix confirm dialog gates the launch only when that step is risky (`failed`/`escalated`/`plan_first`). **Server:** new launch branch `applyCampaignStepBranch` (precedence phaseTaskRef → campaign → **campaignStep** → action → legacy). A body-only `campaignStep:{slug,stepId}` (parsed in `parse-body.ts`, never persisted) is validated (slug `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,128}$`, stepId `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,64}$`, no `..`), the slug existence-checked against the realpath-guarded campaigns dir, and the step's `specPath` resolved via the SAME `readCampaigns` reader the board renders (byte-identical, no second derivation) before `core/launcher.ts buildCopyCommands` builds the command ENTIRELY server-side (Architecture rule 1 / regression guard #19; client never sends a path or command). Errors: `400 invalid_campaign_slug`/`invalid_campaign_step_id`/`campaign_not_found`/`campaign_step_not_found`/`campaign_step_spec_missing`; `campaignStep` mixed with `actionId`/`phaseTaskRef`/`campaignSlug` → `400 mixed_launch_intents`. **No new persisted write surface** — `campaignStep` rides the launch body only; a real resume (JSONL on disk) takes the normal `--resume` shape.

01-adopted::FR-01.37.title: A custom action in `<project>/.shipwright-webui/actions.json` may declare an optional **`slash_command`** field (e.g. `"/content-orchestrator"`) so its `{task.initial_prompt}` placeholder fuses the slash command + flattened task description into ONE shell-quoted positional. **Why:** the Claude CLI accepts a single `[prompt]` positional, so the legacy `{task.description?}` placeholder — which emits the description as a SEPARATE quoted token after the slash command — was silently dropped for custom actions; `{task.initial_prompt}` (the fusing placeholder) was hardcoded to the three bundled action ids (`new-task`/`new-iterate`/`new-pipeline`) and threw `UnknownActionError` for any custom id. **Server:** `core/actions-substitute.ts` threads `SubstitutionContext.slashCommand` from `action.slash_command`; `buildSlashCommand` falls back to it for non-builtin ids (builtins win, SSoT set `BUILTIN_INITIAL_PROMPT_ACTIONS`); both validator and substituter trim before the `SLASH_COMMAND_PATTERN` (`/^\/[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9:_-]*$/`) check. Threaded through the launch branch (`external/launch/action-substitution-branch.ts`) and the GET /actions dry-run (`external/actions/_helpers.ts` + `actions/get.ts`). **Fail-loud at load:** `core/actions-schema-validator.ts` emits `missing_slash_command` / `invalid_slash_command` (`400`) for a non-builtin `{task.initial_prompt}` action without a valid `slash_command` (whitespace-tolerant placeholder detection so `{ task.initial_prompt }` is caught too); a residual `UnknownActionError` at launch is converted to `400 command_substitution_failed`, never a 500. The client does NOT mirror the field (the client never reads it; an uploaded/edited actions.json round-trips `slash_command` via raw JSON). **Server stays the sole substituter. No new write surface.**

01-adopted::FR-01.38.title: The WebUI becomes usable on tablets (768–1023px) **without changing the ≥1024px desktop layout**. A `useIsCompactViewport()` hook (matchMedia `(max-width: 1023px)`) is the single compact-band SSoT. The sidebar auto-rails across the whole compact band (threshold raised 768→1023); the task-board header wraps and its columns (board view) become a scroll-snap touch-swipe carousel (`justify-start`; `lg:` restores `justify-between`); the list view `lg:`-gates the Commit column; the campaigns lane truncates long step titles and wraps the Launch buttons. The task-detail keeps ONE persistent `react-resizable-panels` PanelGroup and, when compact, imperatively sizes the active pane to 100% / others to 0 behind a Files·Session·Viewer `PaneTabBar` — **never swapping the component type at `center`**, so the embedded-terminal subtree is never unmounted across a tab switch or a breakpoint crossing (preserves the WS/scrollback per Architecture rule 21). Bloat-ceiling files (`TaskBoardPage`/`TaskList`/`TaskDetailPage`) stay net-zero via className-only swaps. Phone `<768px` is deferred to a follow-up iterate that reuses the hook + compact primitives.

01-adopted::FR-01.39.title: The WebUI becomes usable on phones (375–480px) **without changing the tablet (768–1023px) or desktop (≥1024px) layouts** — iterate 2 of 2, reusing the FR-01.38 foundation. New `useIsPhoneViewport()` (matchMedia `(max-width: 767px)`) + `useCoarsePointer()` (`(pointer: coarse)`) live alongside `useIsCompactViewport` (no compact-threshold fork). At <768px the sidebar leaves the inline flex flow and becomes an **overlay drawer** hosted in a Radix `Dialog` (a phone-only top-bar hamburger opens it; Escape / scrim / nav-tap close it; full labels; Radix supplies focus-trap + scroll-lock + focus-restore), so content gets the full width. The embedded terminal renders an on-screen **`TerminalKeyBar`** on coarse-pointer (touch) devices — Esc·Tab·Ctrl-C·↑↓←→·Enter·⌨ — writing the exact control bytes to the pty over the EXISTING `socket.send({type:"data"})` writer frame (mode-aware CSI/SS3 arrows via `term.modes.applicationCursorKeysMode`; writer-role re-checked at send time; buttons `preventDefault` so the soft keyboard never drops); the key bar is a sibling of the persistent canvas, so Architecture rule 21 holds. `TaskList` hides the Phase column (`md:`-gate) + scrolls its card; the Projects table scrolls in-card; modals fit (existing `max-w` clamps + 44px touch primary buttons + wrapping footers). `index.html` adds `viewport-fit=cover` + `interactive-widget=resizes-content`; the shell uses `100dvh` + `env(safe-area-inset-*)`; touch targets ≥44px via the `pointer-coarse:` variant. Bloat-ceiling files stay net-zero. **Iterate-2026-06-20 (mobile-terminal-touch-ux) MODIFY:** on a phone (≤767px) the `TaskDetailHeader` is condensed — the breadcrumb + the Started/last-event/model meta sub-line are hidden and padding tightened (via `useIsPhoneViewport`) — to give the embedded terminal more vertical room; tablet/desktop (≥768px) layout is unchanged. **Iterate-2026-06-27 (mobile-modal-touch-safety) MODIFY:** the New-Task / Pipeline / Iterate modal text controls (title, description, project select) carry `pointer-coarse:text-[16px]` so iOS Safari does not auto-zoom (and right-clip) the dialog on focus, and BOTH footer buttons (Save to Backlog + Launch) get the `pointer-coarse:min-h-[44px]` touch target — previously only the primary Launch did, so Launch towered over Save on touch.

01-adopted::FR-01.40.title: The per-project actions.json management surface (Upload .json + Reset-to-default + Custom/Bundled/Malformed badge + validation banners) is now reusable: `ActionsConfigRow` (+ `ResetActionsDialog`) was extracted out of `ActionsConfigCard` with a `hideProjectHeader` flag, rendered **full** on the Settings page (multi-project list) and **compact** in the project edit modal (`ProjectSettingsDialog`, gated on a real non-synthesized project — the dialog already shows name/path). **Bugfix (completes FR-01.37):** the actions-upload route's per-template dry-run now passes `action.slash_command` to `dryRunTemplate` (mirroring `get.ts` incl. its try/catch); uploading a custom `{task.initial_prompt}` + `slash_command` config via Settings returned a 500 and now validates (missing/invalid slash still 400). **Removal:** the stale "Launcher preferences" stub card was deleted from the Settings page (it described a copy-command launcher that no longer exists — Launch/Resume auto-execute via the embedded-terminal header CTA, ADR-068-A1). No new server route (POST/DELETE `/api/projects/:id/actions-upload` already exist); server stays the validation authority.

01-adopted::FR-01.41.title: Density/clipping polish on the two narrow layouts **without changing the ≥1024px desktop layout** (modifies FR-01.38/39). **Phone band (≤767px, `useIsPhoneViewport`):** the Task Board `ProjectFilterDropdown` (new `fluid` prop) moves into the `MainLayout` top bar via `MobileTopBarSlot` — a `useState`-published portal target (re-renders the consumer, no bare-ref race) — and the status filter collapses from a pill row to a funnel-icon Radix `DropdownMenu` (`StatusFilterMenu`; `preventDefault` keeps it open for multi-select). `BoardStatusFilter` holds both `StatusPillRow` (≥768px) + the menu and is extracted out of `TaskBoardPage` to keep it under the bloat ceiling. **Compact band (≤1023px, `useIsCompactViewport`):** the List launch CTA is icon-only (`TerminalLaunchButton` compact label `hidden lg:inline`); the Projects `Path` column is `hidden lg:table-cell` (no bottom scrollbar); the 60px icon-rail count badge overlays the icon (`SidebarNavItem`) instead of clipping; board lanes become flexible (longhand `basis-0 grow`, `min-200`) so all three fit with no right cut-off — `<768px` keeps the snap carousel, `≥1024px` keeps fixed 360px lanes. Bloat-ceiling files stay ≤ their baselines (net-zero / extraction). **Phone-header follow-up (iterate-2026-06-15 phone-header-polish):** the top-bar `fluid` dropdown is content-width (`max-w-[60vw]` + truncate) not full-width; and the All-Projects `+ New` cascade — whose side-opening Radix submenu overflowed off-screen on phones — branches via `useIsPhoneViewport()` to a flat downward drill-down (`ProjectCreatePhoneMenu`: project list → tapping a project REPLACES the popup with its actions → existing NewIssueModal), tablet/desktop keeping the nested cascade.

01-adopted::FR-01.44.title: The embedded terminal is a **faithful truecolor terminal like VS Code's** whose background/foreground/ANSI-16 palette track Claude Code's theme, fixing light-mode rendering (Claude paints no background of its own, so a light Claude theme on the old dark-only palette was black-on-black). **Server:** `spawn-env.ts` supersedes the ADR-067 16-color brand clamp — default `TERM=xterm-256color` + `COLORTERM=truecolor` (no `FORCE_COLOR` cap), so Claude and CLI tools render their own truecolor themes and Claude's `auto` theme detects the xterm background via its OSC 11 query; reversible via `SHIPWRIGHT_TERMINAL_LEGACY_BRAND_COLORS=1` (restores `TERM=dumb`/`COLORTERM=""`/`FORCE_COLOR=1`); `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER` (ADR-095/098) is untouched. `core/claude-theme-reader.ts` reads the `theme` from `~/.claude/settings.json` (read-only — DO-NOT guard #1); `GET /api/terminal/claude-theme` (`routes/terminal-appearance.ts`) returns `{theme}`. **Client:** `terminal-theme.ts` gains a WCAG-AA-tuned `LIGHT_PALETTE` beside the unchanged `DARK_PALETTE` (`paletteFor`); `xterm-theme-options.ts buildXtermTheme(appearance)` is the single ITheme source used at mount AND for the LIVE re-theme (`term.options.theme = <fresh object>`, VS Code's `_updateTheme` pattern; WebGL repaints via `onChangeColors`). `useTerminalAppearance` re-resolves on Claude-theme fetch (mount + focus), Settings toggle (same tab + cross-tab `storage`), and OS `prefers-color-scheme` — no remount (Architecture rule 21). Appearance precedence (`lib/terminalAppearance.ts resolveAppearance`): manual Dark/Light → Settings; System → OS; **Auto (default) → mirror Claude Code** (light\*→light, dark\*→dark, auto→OS, custom/unknown→dark). Persisted per-browser (`lib/terminalPrefs.ts`, like copy-on-selection). Settings gains the Appearance selector (`TerminalSettingsCard`). **No new persisted write surface.** Supersedes ADR-067's brand-fit env clause only (shell-whitelist intact).

01-adopted::FR-01.45.title: In a `single_session` run the design gate is `orchestrator-approve`: the `/shipwright-run` master emits the mockups + the `index.html` review viewer, records a human-gate PAUSE (`.shipwright/run_loop_state.json` → `status: paused_human_gate`) and stops. The Command Center hosts that viewer full-fidelity and captures per-round feedback into the worktree — **no Smart Viewer, no manual export**. **Gate signal (read-only):** `core/run-loop-state-reader.ts` reads `run_loop_state.json` and `deriveDesignGate(loopState, config, hasViewer)` is active ONLY when `status===paused_human_gate` AND `currentPhaseTaskId` resolves to a NON-terminal `phase:"design"` task AND `.shipwright/designs/index.html` exists (the anti-staleness triple). **Server routes** (`external/design-review/{gate,serve,feedback-write,routes}.ts`, mounted like run-config): `GET /api/external/projects/:id/design-gate` → `{active, phaseTaskId, phase}`; `GET /api/external/projects/:id/designs/:rest{.+}` serves the viewer + its relative `screens/*`/`flows/*` assets as **text/html** (NOT the `/file` route's text/plain, and no `nosniff`/`default-src 'none'` that would blank it), path-guarded to the `.shipwright/designs/` subtree, injecting into `index.html` ONLY a bridge that overrides `window.showSaveFilePicker` so the viewer's own `exportFeedback()` posts its markdown to the host; `POST /api/external/projects/:id/design-feedback` writes `.shipwright/designs/design-feedback-round{N}.md` where **N is computed from the round files on disk** (`core/design-feedback.ts computeNextRound`, NOT the viewer's localStorage), the heading round integer normalized to N with every other byte preserved so the monorepo Option-B reader parses it (`normalizeRoundHeading`), `.md`-guarded + size-capped + **exclusive-create with retry** (no two-tab clobber). **Client:** `lib/designReviewApi.ts` + `useDesignGate` (poll while live) → `DesignGatePanel` (paused note + "Review mockups") → `MockupReviewOverlay` (full-bleed Radix Dialog hosting the viewer in a `sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-modals"` iframe; validates `event.origin===location.origin` + `event.source===iframe` before writing; shows "Saved — Round N", panel stays open). **Resume** = the existing `MasterRunLaunchButton` CTA (`claude --resume <uuid>` re-enters the single-session loop, which reads the feedback and revises/finalizes) — **the webui runs no `orchestrator.py` and writes only the transient round-feedback scratch file** (gitignored; never run_config / run_loop_state / Claude JSONL). See FR-01.01 (the `SingleSessionRunCard` conditionally renders `DesignGatePanel`).

01-adopted::FR-01.46.title: A read-only, stateless, tolerant reader over a managed project's DURABLE tracked event log (`<projectRoot>/shipwright_events.jsonl` — the same project-root file `core/campaign-events.ts` reads, NOT under `.shipwright/`), exposing the per-run facts the WOW-usability UI (Mission Control's Record + instruments, Ship's-Log runs/sub-scores/last-proof, board per-run facts) renders. This is the one genuine backend gap in that campaign — `campaign-events.ts` deliberately projects `commit` alone. **Server:** `core/event-log-reader.ts` (`projectEventLog(lines,{runId?})` pure + `readEventLog(projectRoot,opts)` `pathGuard`-resolved file wrapper) + the shape contract `core/event-log-types.ts`. It dedupes `work_completed` by `adr_id` (the task-join key `task.runId==adr_id`; latest by `ts` epoch, file-index tiebreak — parity with `campaign-events.ts`) and projects the full documented record — `runId, eventId, ts, source, intent, changeType, description, summary, commit, specImpact, affectedFrs[], newFrs[], tests{passed,total}, phaseTimings, campaign, subIterateId` — plus phase transitions (`phase_started`/`phase_completed`/`phase_failed`, file-order, uncollapsed, top-level `splitId` read through). **Honest degradation is hard:** every field is optional → `null`/`[]`; `phase_timings` is read THROUGH untouched and comes back `null` when absent (0/217 real runs carry it) — NEVER synthesized/interpolated/back-filled; a torn/half-written line is skipped + counted, never fatal; `event_amended`/`grade_snapshot` are not overlaid (A02 owns the join). `GET /api/external/projects/:projectId/events[?runId=<adr_id>]` (`external/events/routes.ts`, mounted read-only like compliance) returns `{status:"ok", ...projection}`; unknown project → `404 project_not_found`, path-less → `400 project_path_unavailable`; an absent/unreadable log → an ok payload with empty runs + zero counts (graceful). Only documented fields are projected (explicit field-select, never a raw spread — bounds exposure). **No new write surface** (Architecture rule 1 — webui never writes the event log).

01-adopted::FR-01.47.title: A read-only, stateless join that turns FR-01.46's raw event projection into the PER-RUN shape Mission Control / the Ship's-Log gallery / the board consume, plus a grade-trend series. Consumes `core/event-log-reader.ts` (A01) — does NOT re-read or duplicate its dedup. **Join key (highest campaign risk, pinned + tested):** `task.runId == adr_id`, bound at the consumer via the `runId` query param; a task with no runId, or a runId with no matching `work_completed`, degrades to `null` (never guesses). **Server:** `core/run-data-join.ts` (`joinRunData` per-run shape; `deriveGates` DERIVED lamps flagged `derived:true` — only `test` carries a real per-run signal, `review`/`security` honestly `"unknown"`; `projectPhaseDurations` from the iterate flat `phase_timings` mark-list; `aggregatePhaseTransitions` folds pipeline `phase_started`/`phase_completed` by `(phase, splitId)` at PROJECT level — transitions carry no run key so are NEVER pinned to one run, per #369 a phase can have N split-ends; `projectGradeTrend` folds `grade_snapshot` events into an ascending `[{ts,grade,score}]`; `readRunData`/`readRunDetail` `pathGuard`-resolved single-read wrappers) + the shape contract `core/run-data-types.ts`. **Honest degradation is hard (AC3):** durations are NEVER synthesized/interpolated/estimated/back-filled (a negative interval → `null`); a run without `phase_timings` → `phaseDurations:null` (render **n/a**); gate lamps are never an authoritative verdict; the CURRENT grade is NOT re-derived (reuses `core/compliance-reader.ts`) — this adds only the trend. `GET /api/external/projects/:projectId/runs` (list + `pipelinePhaseDurations` + `gradeTrend`), `.../runs/:runId` (detail; an unknown runId → `200 {run:null}`, never a 404/500), `.../grade-trend` (`external/runs/routes.ts`, mounted read-only like events). **Client:** `lib/runDataApi.ts` (its own file — `externalApi.ts` is at the bloat ceiling) + `hooks/useRunData.ts` (React Query, 30 s poll, disabled without ids, verbatim-mirrored types per ADR-080). **No new write surface** (Architecture rule 1).

01-adopted::FR-01.48.title: The Command Center's visual foundation — the **Weather Deck** system every later UI sub-iterate paints on (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A03; precedent that appearance is FR-able here: **FR-01.44**). **Tokens:** a warm-neutral stone ramp + surfaces/text/borders, the deep-teal signature `--accent:#0E7A6B`, semantic colours, elevation, radii and type, plus the v3.1 anthracite/glass tokens (`--glass-light` 122° four-stop sheen gradient, `--glass-filter:blur(20px) saturate(1.3) brightness(1.55)`, `--sh-photo`) and the promoted beige reading surface (`--beige:#ECE4D5`). Exposed to Tailwind v4 utilities via `@theme inline` (referencing the `:root` tokens so the legacy `--color-*` palette in `index.css` is never clobbered — A04 re-points it). **Scene layer:** `SceneBackdrop` reproduces the prototype `applyScene()` DOM contract — a frozen `position:absolute` photo plate + a three-layer scrim + a scrolling `.scene-fore`; wired once in `MainLayout` so **every route** renders ONE signature backdrop (`deck-golden.jpg`; Apple Liquid-Glass model). The wizard route carries `well-left`; First Contact's own hero (`lighthouse.jpg`) plugs into the same seam (A08). **NO imagery tier/band system** — `data-scene-tier` is retracted and `data-depth="band"` is never emitted (guard test). **Glass + `.on-photo` flip AND solid-surface reset:** bare chrome flips light-on-photo (with an inherited legibility text-shadow) while text inside a `.card`/glass panel resets to dark-on-white (shadow cancelled), and secondary text on glass is darkened further — the legibility engineering. **The AA contrast ladder (the primary deliverable):** a machine-checked WCAG-2.1 test over every declared (text-token × worst-case-ground) pair — card `#FFFFFF`, beige, taupe, terminal `#131110`, and two composite grounds (`--ground-glass-worst`, `--ground-photo-worst`) derived once from the real asset — that fails below 4.5:1 (body) / 3:1 (large + UI). The four measured Fable-B5 failures (brand tag 2.90, terminal dim 3.65–3.92, `--faint` as body text 2.52, dismissed-triage `opacity:.6`) are each fixed or explicitly re-roled and the test proves it (`--faint` is re-roled NON-TEXT). **Appearance-only: no new persisted write surface, no data, no action wiring** (Architecture rule 1 / DO-NOT #11/#12). Terminal xterm bytes/theme are untouched (ADR-097 / FR-01.44).

01-adopted::FR-01.49.title: A third npm workspace `bootstrapper/` (scoped package `@svenroth-ai/shipwright`; the bare `shipwright` name is taken on npm) makes ONE command install AND update the whole system — the `/shipwright-*` plugins AND the Command Center, first run and every run after (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A06; change_type=feature). **Order** (`bin/shipwright.mjs`): self-version check → preflight → plugins(+cache-sync) → server → summary. **Preflight** (`lib/preflight.mjs`): claude, uv, a WORKING Python (TEST-RUN `--version` — rejects the Microsoft-Store `python3` alias stub, which `command -v` finds but which reports no version), Node ≥ 20.12, git; a missing hard prerequisite is a loud, non-zero refusal, never a dead install that looks fine. **Plugins** (`lib/plugins.mjs` pure + `lib/claude-cli.mjs` OS seams): the list is DERIVED from the marketplace manifest (local cache → GitHub raw → `SHIPWRIGHT_MARKETPLACE_MANIFEST`; NEVER a hardcoded array — the README said "13" while the manifest lists 14), defensively validated (a bad shape is a loud named error, never a silent empty list — the manifest is now a PUBLISHED CROSS-REPO CONTRACT); install (first run) / update (rerun); before/after name→version snapshot drives the restart notice EXACTLY when the set changed. **Cache-sync** (`lib/cache-sync.mjs` + `lib/cache-runtime.mjs`): ports `update-marketplace.sh` BEHAVIOUR sans its hardcoded list — the make-or-break `shared/` sync into the cache root (without it every plugin hook `uv run "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/../../shared/…"` dies at session start), full file sync, the `plugins/` symlink layer with a Windows copy fallback, GC of stale version dirs, and a coherence post-condition that fails loud on a half-synced cache. **Server** (`lib/server.mjs`): probe `127.0.0.1:PORT/api/diagnostics` (TCP occupancy first, then HTTP identity by `app.name` + `app.version`) — free→boot the packaged (built) server, same/newer→attach (no 2nd server), older→SWAP via the DETACHED `deploy-swap.mjs` (safe from inside the Command Center's own terminal), foreign→loud fail with a `PORT=` remediation and the incumbent left ALIVE (NEVER killed — PR #249). **Server change (the only in-scope `server/src` edit):** `/api/diagnostics` additively exposes `app:{name,version}` (the attach-vs-swap signal). **`npm publish` is a MANUAL human gate** — no publish attempted, no `NPM_TOKEN`, no publish workflow. Architecture fences: drives the `claude` plugin CLI as an INSTALLER, never spawns a `claude` session, never writes `~/.claude/projects/**` / `shipwright_run_config.json` / `run_loop_state.json` (rule 1 / DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.50.title: One plain-language bank of the jargon the Command Center surfaces, each term with a ONE-LINE explanation shown exactly where it appears (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A07; change_type=feature). **Glossary** (`client/src/lib/glossary.ts`): plain data + a case-insensitive `glossaryLookup` (no component logic). Framework terms (IREB, ADR, RTM, SBOM, Gate, Harness, Conventional Commits) copy the monorepo Plain-Language Index (`shipwright/docs/guide.md` Appendix A) WORD-FOR-WORD; the two mid-run prompt glosses (AskUserQuestion, approval gate) copy the approved prototype (`Spec/prototype/screens/inbox.js`) verbatim; FR / Spec / Canon and the ten pipeline phases get honest concise entries. A verbatim-guard test (`glossary.test.ts`) pins every framework/prototype string so a reword is caught, not merged — one source, no drift. **Teaching empty states** (one sentence + exactly one action each): Inbox (`InboxPage.tsx`, the verbatim prototype card + the "approval gate" gloss + a "Go to the board" CTA), Task Board (`TaskBoardEmptyState.tsx`, shown by `TaskBoardPage.tsx` only when the active project has NO tasks — a status filter that merely hides tasks falls through to the columns), Projects (`ProjectsPage.tsx`, the verbatim prototype logbook line + Create Project CTA). **JIT tooltips:** the create-dialog phase chips (`NewIssueModal/PhaseDropdown.tsx`) surface each phase's one-liner as a `title`. **Honest by construction (AC3):** all copy is static — no fake live data; nothing the prototype tags `backend`/`demo` ships claiming to be live; the empty states render only when a surface is genuinely empty. The optional dev-only "Data map" provenance overlay is SCOPED OUT (a useful overlay needs `data-prov` on values across surfaces + a sidebar toggle, all outside this iterate's footprint). **No new write surface; read-only observer** (Architecture rule 1 / DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.51.title: A guided three-door entry placed BEFORE the expert `ProjectWizard` (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A08; change_type=feature). **One state machine, one flight-plan rail, one step-dot — three doors feeding them** (`client/src/components/wizard/IntentWizard/`, every file ≤300 LOC; `wizardState.ts` reducer + row derivation). **New** — the 4 plain-language questions (brief + example chips · users · remember · runs-at) → the plan card with the 7 pipeline phases; the live flight-plan rail grows one "Because you said X → Y" row per answer and shows unanswered fields as dim spine nodes (not `—` rows). **Adopt** — folder pick (+ recent-path chips) → a real WORKING screen (lists the actual steps; nothing written/uploaded) → the two-column result (what's here / what adopt writes) ending in a Mission task. **Grade** — folder-OR-GitHub-URL pick → WORKING (shows the shallow-clone step for a remote) → the Control-Grade card that renders the shipwright-grade **`ReportModel` field-for-field** (ring + four dimensions + per-dimension structured `provenance` + `honest_ceiling_note` above the dims + per-dimension `would_light_up` + `network_enrichments` receipt); **the honest `n/a` is load-bearing — `status` drives the visual, no code path synthesizes a score for an underivable dimension** (`reportShape.ts` REFUSES a synthesized-score/unknown-major payload → an honest "shape not recognised" state; guard tests bite). Grade → "Adopt this repo →" converts to the adopt result WITHOUT re-asking the folder. **The readiness line is a GATE, not a greeting** — `GET /api/readiness` (`server/src/{routes/readiness.ts,core/readiness-probe.ts}`) re-expresses the bootstrapper preflight set server-side (Claude CLI ≥ min · plugins installed · coherent `cache/shipwright/shared/` · uv · a TEST-RUN Python ≥ 3.11 · git — "one truth, two surfaces" with A06/A14); not-ready → the three doors are INERT (disabled buttons, not merely dimmed), the missing checks + why + the one repair command (`npx @svenroth-ai/shipwright@latest`) are named, and a probe error errs to not-ready. **No demo toggle ships** (`__fcDemo` guarded absent). Deep links `/wizard`, `/wizard/adopt`, `/wizard/grade` (`router.tsx`); the board empty-state CTA opens the wizard (`TaskBoardPage.tsx`); a quiet "Already set up? Add the existing project" line routes to the classic registration (`/projects`), not a 4th door. **A08 is UI-only** — scan/grade data is a provenance-tagged STUB shown as an explicit not-live placeholder (A09 wires the real `/shipwright-adopt` · `/shipwright-grade`); additive (the expert `ProjectWizard` stays reachable, unchanged); read-only observer (Architecture rule 1 / DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.52.title: A09a (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`; change_type=feature) makes A08's stubbed New + Adopt doors REAL and writes down the data contract that A08 left implicit (Codex rebuild gap #3). **Grade stays on A08's provenance-tagged stub — A09b owns its server route** (the deliberate client/server split that de-escalated the "two architectures" A09). **Data contract (the deliverable, `contract.ts`):** the per-door REQUEST body (New/Adopt/Grade), the CREATE response (`projectId`/`taskId`/`sessionUuid`), and the LAUNCH-state union (`idle`/`creating`/`created`/`launching`/`running`/`launch-failed`/`jsonl-missing`/`permission-denied`, the last two DEFINED for A17's post-navigation transcript state machine). Pure builders map a request → the exact create/task/launch payloads and GUARANTEE `actionId` + a brief on every New/Adopt launch — **the single break the contract exists to prevent: a launch WITHOUT an `actionId` falls through to the legacy `buildCopyCommands()` path and lands an EMPTY prompt** (asserted per door). **New** — the 4 answers map silently onto registration: StackProfile from Q3 (`remember=Yes`→`supabase-nextjs` + free-account note; else `vite-hono`, the zero-signup local default — single SSoT `resolveStackProfile`, `profileFor` delegates); Supabase env vars flagged ONLY for "web + remember" (surfaced as a plan-card note + folded into the intake brief, never collected here — the Deploy phase asks for the real secrets); the plan card gains a name (prefilled slug) + a required target-folder input (a project needs a home) → create project (POST /projects) → create task (`new-pipeline`) → launch (POST /tasks/:id/launch → `/shipwright-run:run`) with the FULL intake as the brief (so the terminal interview doesn't re-ask) → `webui:pending-auto-launch:<taskId>` sessionStorage handoff → navigate to Mission. **Adopt** — the picked LOCAL folder → register the project → launch `new-task` + the one-shot `adopt` phase (→ `/shipwright-adopt`) with the repo path as the brief; a remote URL (grade→adopt handoff / github chip) is refused with an honest "clone it first" note (adopt registers a local repo — no client-side clone). Adopt copy softened to match the plugin: never-overwrite, originals kept byte-for-byte — no diff-approval UI is claimed (AC5). Grade → "Adopt this repo →" carries the same path into the adopt launch without re-asking. **Launch fails CLOSED** at each step (create-project / create-task / launch) with a distinct reason + an honest launch-failed screen whose "Try again" re-runs the same request. **Webui spawns no Claude** — the command is built ENTIRELY server-side (`actions-substitute.ts` shell-escapes all user-derived path/brief tokens; Architecture rule 1 / regression guard #19). Additive: the expert `ProjectWizard` / `CreateMenuSplitButton` are unaffected; read-only observer (DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.53.title: A09b (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`; change_type=feature) wires A08's stubbed Grade door to the REAL `shipwright-grade` plugin via a **read-only** server route, consuming the `GradeRequest` + `GradeReportState` shapes A09a wrote into `contract.ts` (the deliberate client/server split that de-escalated the "two architectures" A09). **Server (IO/injection boundary — the reason A09 was split):** `POST /api/wizard/grade` (`routes/grade.ts` → `core/grade-runner.ts` + `core/grade-target.ts`) runs `python grade.py <target> --format json` with **`execFile`, shell:false, a fixed-literal python binary** (TEST-RUN-resolved python3→python→py, reusing `readiness-probe.resolvePython`) and the target as a **fixed positional after a `--` end-of-options separator** — defeating BOTH shell and argument injection (a `--no-clone`-style target can never be read as a flag). The versioned plugin cache is resolved via `shipwrightCacheRoot` + `compareVersions` (`<cache>/shipwright-grade/<ver>/scripts/tools/grade.py`); the `shipwright-compliance` engine root is passed via `SHIPWRIGHT_GRADE_COMPLIANCE_ROOT` (grade.py's engine_bridge locator, which the versioned cache layout otherwise can't find). **Target validation** (shape + existence, NOT path-confinement — grade may grade any repo): empty/oversized/NUL/implausible-URL/embedded-credentials rejected, and an SSRF guard blocks loopback/private/link-local/CGNAT IP-literal remote hosts (an exposed webui can't be turned into a `git clone` metadata-endpoint probe). **READ-ONLY:** no project registration, no writes, never touches `~/.claude/projects/**` / `shipwright_run_config.json` / `run_loop_state.json` (rules 1/12); a remote URL is shallow-cloned + purged BY grade.py, not webui. **Honest outcomes (never a fabricated grade):** exit 0 + JSON → `report-ready` (the RAW `ReportModel`, shape-guarded on the CLIENT by `reportShape.parseReportModel` — the cross-repo contract check, ADR-045), exit 2 → `grade-failed`, exit 3 / python-or-script-missing → `engine-unavailable` (reusing the readiness repair command), non-JSON → `shape-unrecognised`. **Client:** `useGradeReport.ts` (fetch + map to `GradeReportState`, fires only once a target is committed) feeds `GradeResult.tsx`, replacing A08's `GRADE_REPORT` stub with real output + an honest state for grading / grade-failed / engine-unavailable / shape-unrecognised. **`n/a` pass-through (load-bearing):** an underivable dimension arrives `score:null` and renders `n/a` — no client default/estimate/fallback fills it (AC5). **Scale fix (round-trip finding):** real dimension `score`/`weight` are 0..1 fractions (the plugin renders `weight*100`% + `score:.2f`), scaled ×100 for the 0..100 bar/value idiom (`GradeDimensionRow`), never applied to an n/a null; the stub is aligned to the real wire scale. The `/wizard/grade` visual baseline moves from the step-1 repo-pick to the real Control-Grade card (deterministic `/api/wizard/grade` E2E fixture). Additive; read-only observer (rule 1 / DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.54.title: A pure, side-effect-free client library that maps the per-run facts A01/A02 already read (FR-01.46/FR-01.47) plus the two phase-rail structures onto the APPROVED plain-language copy, for the Mission-Control surfaces A11–A13 render (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A10; change_type=feature). **Deterministic mapping (AC1):** the same event always yields the same sentence; every string is lifted **VERBATIM** from the clickable prototype (the design SSoT — the old "wording-go" human gate is retired) with its real code points preserved byte-for-byte (`“”` U+201C/D, `’` U+2019, `—` U+2014, `→` U+2192, `·` U+00B7) — glosses from `Spec/prototype/screens/wizard.js planCard()`, verdict + mission lines + The Record captions from `screens/taskDetail.js`, the two rails from `data.js`. A verbatim-guard test pins them against INDEPENDENT literals so a reword is caught, not merged. **Two rails, different in kind (AC2):** the **pipeline rail** is the real 7 orchestrator phases `project → design → plan → build → test → changelog → deploy` — there is **NO "secure" node** (security is decoupled + CI/compliance-sourced, `SECURITY_LAMP_SOURCE`, `isPipelinePhase("secure")===false`); the **iterate rail** is a 5-node DISPLAY grouping `Scope → Build → Review → Test → Finalize` DERIVED from `sessionPlan.phases[].group` (membership read from the mapping, ordered by the authoritative `iterateGroups` order; an unknown/`secure` group is never injected as a 6th node — never a hardcoded phase list). **Honest degradation (AC3):** an absent count/outcome/duration renders an explicit `n/a` or the clause is dropped — the narrator NEVER invents a number, count, outcome or duration it did not read (0 is a read value, not "absent" — strict `!= null`); a fields-stripped fixture asserts no fabricated digit appears. **Provenance honesty (AC4):** every value narrated is a `reader`/`derivable` fact from A01/A02 (`RunDataJoin`, gates carry `derived:true`) or degrades to an honest empty state — nothing tagged `backend`/`demo` is narrated as live. **Library only — NO UI change → no visual-regression baseline moves** (A11–A13 turn the strings into pixels). No server change; no write surface; read-only observer (Architecture rule 1 / DO-NOT #12).

01-adopted::FR-01.58.title: The highest-stakes moment (nothing builds until you approve) rebuilt as the SAME three-card Mission layout instead of a foreign screen (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A14; change_type=feature). **Reuses A13's shell (AC1):** in `designgate` mode (from A11's `useMissionState`) A12's `OperationCard` routes its middle `.mc-op` slot to `DesignGateCard` — NO new page/route/header/glass recipe; the Record rail (Design node `now`, later nodes `pending`) + artifact panel are A11's, unchanged; the old board-card `DesignGatePanel` is reduced to a thin, non-competing "awaiting approval" hint. **Read-only observer (AC2 / FR-01.45 / DO-NOT #12):** the gate WRITES nothing to `run_loop_state.json`, `shipwright_run_config.json`, or Claude's JSONL — a source-scan test proves no write path from the gate surface to run-loop/run-config; the ONLY write is the transient gitignored `design-feedback-round{N}.md` via the EXISTING `feedback-write.ts` (round derived from disk). The screen list is READ from the design phase's `design-manifest.md` through the existing generic `/file` route (no new server surface). **Approve = the existing CTA path (AC3, Architecture rule 1):** the WebUI spawns/drives nothing and never invokes `orchestrator.py`; Approve runs the SAME `useLaunchTask` + `LaunchCoordinator` resume dispatch as the header ResumeCTA (server builds the command via `core/launcher.ts`, the embedded terminal auto-executes it after an explicit, DELIBERATE two-step confirm) — a failed resume surfaces honestly, never silently flipping the badge. **Exactly one primary (AC4):** at the gate the header Resume CTA is SUPPRESSED (resume stays reachable in the `⋯` menu), so Approve is the sole primary. **Real previews (AC5):** each gallery card is a real hosted-preview iframe (the emitted `screens/*.html` via the existing `serve.ts`) + FR id + name, the whole tile a keyboard-reachable button opening the real viewer; a row with no file → an honest per-card placeholder; gate open + zero screens → an honest empty state, never a fabricated gallery. **Derived, not latched (AC6):** when the `useDesignGate` poll shows `paused_human_gate` cleared, Mission returns to A12 with no manual refresh. **Provenance honesty (AC7):** Tests/Serves chips render their honest empty state at a gate (a gate precedes tests — a green "1882/1882" chip would be a lie). **A11y (AC9):** dark-on-glass text ≥4.5:1, gallery keyboard-navigable, focus rings on Approve/Request-changes, "Waiting on you" = badge+text not colour alone. Motion is A20. Only the `/tasks/:taskId` (Mission tab, designgate) visual baseline moves (A00's `design-gate` route flips `pending`→`baselined`).

A RESTYLE, never a rewrite — every existing behaviour is preserved. **Top row (`MissionTopRow`, `.mc-top`):** back arrow → board; a **"Board › \<Project\>" breadcrumb with BOTH segments clickable** (Board → `/`, project → the projects surface until A16 ships the Ship's Log); the title + inline rename + the RICH task-state badge (lossless — all 7 states) with an ADDITIVE "Awaiting approval" pill when `useMissionState` is `designgate`; A11's Instruments; the Resume CTA (hidden when `done`); and the `⋯` **HeaderMenu with EVERY item intact** (rename, edit, copy session UUID, copy Resume command, move to project, backlog / re-open, close, stop terminal session, delete, clear terminal history, session-details toggle) — a restyle that drops a menu item is a regression. `TaskDetailHeader` now DELEGATES to `MissionTopRow` (stable import + testids preserved for the whole test corpus). **Tab row (`MissionTabRow` + reusable `MissionSegmented`):** the segmented Mission

01-adopted::FR-01.59.title: The Projects registry, formerly a six-column table (`ProjectsPage`), rebuilt as a Ship's-Log GALLERY of preview cards (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A15; change_type=feature). Each project is a `<ProjectLogCard>` in a `.log-gallery` grid (`auto-fill, minmax(330px, 1fr)`): colour dot · name · grade pill (the existing `ComplianceGradeBadge`, FR-01.43) · mono path · body · foot (gear + trash, exact prior behaviour + testids, · task count · "Open board ›"). **Three REAL states (AC2), never fabricated (AC3):** the card body is derived by `client/src/lib/projectLogStats.ts` (`buildProjectLogModel`) from A02's per-run join (`useProjectRuns`/`RunsResponse`, FR-01.47) keyed by project id + the compliance score — a GRADED project (A02 returns runs) renders a runs sparkline (`ProjectRunSparkline`, plain `<i>` bars, NO chart lib — grade-trend scores, else per-run test-ratio, else a unit bar per run) + `N runs · M FRs · SS/100` + the most-recent run's summary as a proof quote; an UNGRADED project (registered, zero runs) renders the honest `.lc-empty` sentence ("No runs yet. Grade or adopt it to open the logbook.") and NEVER a zeroed sparkline — EXCEPT a non-Shipwright project (sessions present, zero Shipwright runs) which shows its session count ("N sessions — open the log to view them.") instead of the grade/adopt nudge (Sven 2026-07-17, iterate-2026-07-17-ui-polish-forms-shiplog); a many-project board sorts GRADED-FIRST (`useQueries` fans out one run bundle per project, stable decorate-sort). The prototype's `logStats()` demo literals (`209`, `43/43 FRs`, the 16-value spark array) appear NOWHERE — an empty A02 payload returns `{ graded: false }`. **"Open board" is honest (AC4, A16 pairing):** the card click + `.lc-open` route through the SINGLE `openProjectLog()` seam (`client/src/lib/projectNav.ts`) to the surface that exists TODAY — the board filtered to the project (`/?projectId=…`, what a row click always did); there is NO placeholder `/projects/:id/log` route (a dead route is a lie with a URL) — A16 re-points that one function. Labelled "Open board" (not "Open log") because the interim destination is the board, not a logbook. **"Grade it" never dead-ends (AC5):** an ungraded card with no compliance renders a real CTA that navigates to the existing read-only Grade door (`/wizard/grade`, FR-01.51) — the WebUI spawns/drives nothing (Architecture rule 1) and hardcodes no slash-command/phase string (DO-NOT #11). Read-only observer: no `run_config` write (DO-NOT #12), no `state`/`boardColumn` touch (rule 23), no Claude JSONL write (DO-NOT #1). `ProjectsPage` SHRINKS (452 → ~200); every new file ≤ its cap; bloat baseline not ratcheted. Only the `/projects` visual baseline moves.

01-adopted::FR-01.60.title: A project's HOME becomes its logbook: the new route `/projects/:projectId/log` → `<ShipsLogPage>` (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A16; change_type=feature). **One nav seam (AC1):** A15's single `openProjectLog()` (`client/src/lib/projectNav.ts`) is RE-POINTED from the interim `/?projectId=…` board destination to this real route — one edit, no second path, no leftover destination; the `ProjectLogCard` affordance is renamed "Open board" → "Open log". **The Captain's Drawer (AC2, `CaptainsDrawer`):** a 76px `.glass-card` grade strip — the grade RING is REUSED from the wizard's `GradeRing` (`.gring`, FR-01.51/A11), NOT a third ring — with an eyebrow ("the captain's drawer · why A · 98/100") and inline sub-scores that render **only** when the reader parsed the dimension table; **no bars at all** when it did not (a full bar for a dashboard we could not parse is a fabricated claim). "Why an A?" opens the REAL control record by REUSING `ComplianceDetailModal` (FR-01.43), not the prototype's file://-safe inline panel. The one net-new datum: `server/src/core/compliance-dimensions.ts` (split out of `compliance-reader.ts` to hold both under 300 LOC) parses the Control-Verdict table into `dimensions:{key,label,value,pct,doc}[]` — `pct` is the dashboard's OWN ✅/⚠️/❌ per-dimension verdict as a bar height, NEVER a fraction scraped from the free-text signal; `controlVerdictMarkdown` is left untouched (the modal still renders it verbatim); an absent/malformed table → `[]`, never a throw. **The logbook (AC3, `LogEntryList`):** one `.logentry` per run from A02 (`useProjectRuns`, FR-01.47) — date · intent badge · summary · FR pill (`+N` overflow) · derived gate dots (only when A02 reports outcomes) · 7-char commit · chevron. THE JOIN IS THE RISK: a row maps to a task by `runId == task.runId`; a run WITH a joined task is a clickable entry into `/tasks/:taskId`, a run WITHOUT one is a NON-clickable `<div>` — never a router push into a 404. A project with no readable event log renders an honest empty sheet, NEVER the prototype's demo rows (`209 runs`, `1882/1882`, the three hardcoded green dots appear NOWHERE). **Non-Shipwright projects (Sven 2026-07-17):** a project with NO Shipwright runs but existing SESSIONS (custom-action projects, e.g. Content Marketing, that never emit a Shipwright event log) lists its recent sessions (date · state · title, most-recent first, each clickable into `/tasks/:taskId`) instead of the empty sheet; a project with neither runs nor sessions uses the honest "No runs yet. Grade or adopt it to open the logbook." nudge. Sessions are the project's tasks (`useExternalTasks`, real observer data), never fabricated. **The scoped-iterate promptbox (AC4, `ScopedIteratePromptbox`):** "What do you want to change?" (auto-focused on load) → a scoped plan card whose unfillable fields (complexity · affected FRs · risk flags · est. tests · phases) render `—`, never a plausible guess (there is no client sizing engine) → one Go that creates + launches through the EXISTING path — the action id comes from the actions manifest (`useProjectActions`, `new-iterate`), NEVER a hardcoded slash-command/phase (DO-NOT #11); the command is server-built (`core/launcher.ts`) and auto-executed by the embedded terminal via `writePendingAutoLaunch` after the explicit CTA (Architecture rule 1 / DO-NOT #19); the WebUI spawns nothing. **The graduation card (`GraduationCard`):** the dismissable "Baseline set <date>" proof line, shown only when a real earliest-run date exists (never a fabricated "Baseline set —"), dismissal persisted per-project. **Board-card hygiene (AC5, Sven's call):** a standing guard `TaskCard.hygiene.test.tsx` proves a board card carries NO FR chips and NO gate dots even when A02 makes that data available — RED the moment either is re-added; `TaskCard.tsx` LOC goes down (538 < 543), never ratchets. Read-only observer throughout: no `run_config`/`run_loop_state` write (DO-NOT #12), no `state`/`boardColumn` touch (rule 23), no Claude JSONL write (DO-NOT #1). Motion (ring/trend/entry) is A20. Visual baselines: `ships-log` flips `pending`→`baselined` (owner A16); the board card render is byte-unchanged so its baseline does NOT move.

01-adopted::FR-01.63.title: When Claude pauses mid-run to ask (`AskUserQuestion`, a `text_question`, a `terminal_prompt` like `Overwrite existing migration…? (y/N)`), the Inbox SHOWS the question and offers a clear hand-off: the operator is taken to the task's embedded terminal, focused and ready, where **they** type the reply — the WebUI never answers for them (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A19, DECIDED BY SVEN 2026-07-14; change_type=feature). This ships the SAFE half only. **The fence, proven as code (AC1):** the Inbox surface contains NO control that writes bytes into a live session — no text input / textarea / contentEditable, no terminal-write import, no WS send, no clipboard copy (`inbox-no-writepath.test.ts` grep-scans `pages/inbox/**` + `InboxPage.tsx`); A00's terminal byte-path guard stays green with outbound frames byte-identical to `main` — this FR adds ZERO bytes to any pty. Options render as **read-only text**, never clickable option buttons that could send. The deferred write-path lives at triage `trg-475219a0` — NOT built here. **The CTA is a navigation (AC2, `InboxResumeButton` clipboard→navigation):** "Answer in the terminal" navigates to `/tasks/<taskId>` via a single deep-link module (`client/src/lib/taskDeepLink.ts` builds `?pane=terminal&focus=terminal`; `TaskDetailPage` reads it, selects Files & Terminal + the Terminal segment, and focuses the terminal through the EXISTING pendingFocus/attach path — no second attach path — then strips the query so a reload does not re-snap); proven by E2E landing on the focused terminal (the next keystroke is the operator's). **Anatomy for all three kinds (AC3, from the prototype):** amber left strip · context pill · time-ago · glossed `AskUserQuestion` eyebrow · question · rationale · `Options:` line as read-only text where the payload has options; the `terminal_prompt` mono block keeps its recessed panel; each waiting card carries the navigation CTA + the honesty line. **Honesty copy (AC4):** every waiting card states plainly, in a test-asserted string, that the operator answers in the terminal and the WebUI does not answer for them; A07's glossary is reused, not re-authored. **Contrast (AC5, §5.2 / A03 ladder):** the populated list sits on a SOLID neutral sub-panel (the on-photo flipping `--ink`/`--muted` reset to the stone ramp) so body text reads ≥4.5:1, never over the bright photo; A07's teaching empty state is kept. **Provenance honesty (AC6):** the prototype's `__inboxAnswer` mock ("typed into the running terminal") ships in NO form; the Inbox renders only real items from `useExternalInbox`/`useInboxData` or the honest empty state. Read-only observer throughout (rules 1/12 / DO-NOT #1): the WebUI spawns/writes nothing; server surface unchanged. Every new/changed file ≤300 LOC; bloat baseline not ratcheted; `doc-sync.test.ts` green. Visual baselines: `/inbox` empty is unchanged; a new `inbox-populated` route is added (orchestrator regenerates the pinned-container PNG).

01-adopted::FR-01.62.title: The Files & Terminal task-detail body is restyled into three cards on the signature backdrop (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A18; change_type=feature): **Files** (left, `.ft-card.ft-files`, GLASS) · **Terminal/Transcript** (middle, `.ft-card.ft-term`, **SOLID warm beige** — `background: var(--surface-reading)`, `backdrop-filter: none`, border `--surface-reading-line`; glass on the middle card is a bug) · **Smart Preview** (right, `.ft-card.ft-view`, GLASS). Each card carries a greyed `.ft-head` band (`rgba(41,37,34,.17)` on-photo) the segmented tabs sit INSIDE — the middle head holds the Transcript‧Terminal segment (`.mc-tabs.ft-seg`, reusing A13's `MissionSegmented`; the center KEEPS Radix `role="tab"` so the whole terminal E2E corpus's `getByRole("tab",{name:/terminal/i})` stays at one match) + a **Maximize terminal** control (`FocusModeToggle`), the right head the file tabs (`ViewerTabBar`). **THE FENCE — the pty byte path is UNTOUCHED (AC1, load-bearing):** the restyle is CSS-only around the terminal; `client/src/components/terminal/**`, `server/src/terminal/**`, `useAutoLaunch.ts` and the `client/package.json` xterm pins have ZERO diff vs `main`, so A00's outbound-WS byte-path guard (`terminal-byte-path-guard.spec.ts`) stays green unmodified. **NO REMOUNT (AC4):** the existing `react-resizable-panels` shell (`TaskDetailThreePane`) is preserved — resize / persist / collapse / keyboard splitters intact; "maximize" is a TRANSIENT `useThreePaneLayout.maximized` that reuses the collapse→resize path (which fires the pty resize the terminal needs — no new hide path, no 120-col desync) via `FocusModeContext`; a unit test proves the center subtree mounts once and never unmounts across collapse / maximize / drag, and the E2E proves the REAL pty survives + its outbound frames are unchanged. **Fences asserted as code (AC3):** `a18-terminal-fences.test.ts` pins the xterm 6.0.0-family exact pins (no carets), `convertEol:false`, `windowsMode` absent, and the pty spawn whitelist (shells, never `claude`). **Provenance honesty (AC7):** the shipped cards render the REAL FolderTree · REAL terminal/transcript · REAL SmartViewer or an honest empty state — the prototype's `.termbig` scrollback, "Session ended" banner and `.ft-code` demo strings appear NOWHERE. The terminal keeps its OWN dark canvas (`useTerminalAppearance`/`xterm-theme-options.ts`, FR-01.44) — the beige is the CARD, not the terminal. Read-only observer throughout (rules 1/12 / DO-NOT #1/#17/#19): the WebUI spawns nothing; auto-execute is the client-side WS data-frame after an explicit CTA. Every new/changed file ≤300 LOC (the shell's two verbatim splitter handles extracted to `PaneSplitter` to hold the line); bloat baseline not ratcheted; `doc-sync.test.ts` green. Visual baseline: only `/tasks/:taskId` Files & Terminal (`task-detail-terminal`, terminal pane masked).
