01-adopted::FR-01.64.title: The Command Center gets a motion SYSTEM and, as a **behavioural requirement**, a `prefers-reduced-motion` contract (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A20; change_type=feature). **THE CONTRACT (load-bearing, behavioural — AC1):** `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is the PRIMARY user's everyday state (Sven runs Windows with animations off), NOT an accessibility edge case. Under reduced motion EVERY route that exists renders its **COMPLETE FINAL state** — all content present, fully opaque, at its final position, immediately: counters show their final number, rings/sparklines show their final path, staggered lists show ALL items, open slide-overs are on-screen. A component whose content only becomes visible via an animation is a **BUG** that fails this FR — content is NEVER hidden by default and revealed by a keyframe; the pattern is to animate FROM a visible-safe resting state (base CSS is the final state) or gate the animation, never the content. Asserted, not assumed: a Playwright walk forces `emulateMedia({reducedMotion:'reduce'})` over the A00 manifest routes + a Vitest `matchMedia` mock renders each animated primitive → final, visible; the `visual` project now emulates `reducedMotion:'reduce'` so the baselines ARE the reduced-motion final state. **The primitives (AC2/AC5):** a four-step duration ladder + named easings + a CAPPED stagger utility live in `client/src/lib/motion.ts` (TS constants) mirrored by `client/src/styles/motion.css` (CSS custom props + shared earned keyframes + the global reduced-motion FLOOR that neutralises motion app-wide — a FLOOR that stops motion, NOT the contract that makes content appear); the JS-driven moments read `useReducedMotion` (fails toward NO motion when `matchMedia` is absent — never toward hidden content) / `useCountUp` (final value immediately under reduce). NO animation library is added (`framer-motion`/`react-spring`/GSAP) — CSS custom props + transitions/keyframes + WAAPI only; bloat baseline not ratcheted. **Only the earned moments animate (AC2), and only they:** the Mission proof lines (`ProofSummary` stagger-in), the artifact slide-over (`mission-record.css` token slide-in), the grade ring draw (`GradeRing` count-up), the Ship's-Log entries (`LogEntryList` stagger-in) — token application, no behaviour change beyond the motion; decorative motion (hover-bounces, backdrop parallax/Ken Burns, page swooshes, per-card shimmer) is out of scope by design; the backdrop stays static. **THE TERMINAL FENCE (AC3):** no animation/transition/transform/filter on the terminal host or its ancestors; `client/src/components/terminal/**` has ZERO diff; the terminal/replay corpus + the WebGL atlas specs stay green; any Files-&-Terminal cross-fade is opacity on the pane body only (PRs #146/#175/#194/#206/#248). **Auto-scroll intact (AC4):** `useAutoScroll` + CSS-first `overflow-anchor` unchanged (DO-NOT #2); no `scroll-behavior: smooth` on the transcript; no scroll library. **Provenance honesty (AC8):** motion never manufactures liveness — no pulse/stream/count-up over data that is stubbed/absent/`n/a` (a null grade has no arc and no count-up). Read-only observer throughout (rules 1/12 / DO-NOT #1); every new/changed file ≤300 LOC; `doc-sync.test.ts` green. Visual baselines: a pure-motion change moves ZERO baselines (A00 captures animations-disabled; the reduced-motion final state == the animations-off final state).

01-adopted::FR-01.65.title: The fast pro-tool layer — a command palette, a consistent keyboard model, density control, focus modes and cache-first rendering (campaign `webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10`, A21; change_type=feature). **THE FENCE — global shortcuts INERT in the terminal + text entry (AC1, load-bearing):** one predicate `isTypingContext()` (owned by `client/src/hooks/useKeyboardMap.ts`, shared by every keyboard surface) disables every global binding when focus is inside the xterm host, an `<input>`/`<textarea>`/`<select>`/contenteditable, or an open dialog, and when `event.isComposing` (IME); a key that is not handled is NEVER `preventDefault()`ed. Even the palette open chord (`Ctrl/⌘+K`) is inert inside the terminal, so `^K` and every letter reach the pty byte-identical to `main` — A00's terminal byte-path guard stays green; this iterate adds ZERO bytes to any pty and `client/src/components/terminal/**` + `server/src/terminal/**` have ZERO diff (rule 1 / DO-NOT #17/#19). Proven by a Vitest fence (`useKeyboardMap.test.tsx`, incl. the IME case) AND a real-browser flow. **The palette (AC2, `CommandPalette` on `@radix-ui/react-dialog` — NO cmdk/hotkey lib):** rendered GLASS on the Weather-Deck system — the A03 recipe `--glass-light` + `--glass-filter` + `--glass-light-line` + `--sh-photo` (`styles/command-center.css`, asserted in a test AND a new `palette-open` visual baseline) — the backdrop visible through it; fuzzy filter over a registry grouped Jump/Open/Launch/Filter, Arrow (and Ctrl+J/K) to move, Enter to run, Esc to close, recent-first. **One source (AC4, `client/src/lib/commandRegistry.ts`):** the palette, the `?` cheat-sheet (`ShortcutsSheet`) and menus read ONE registry — every binding is in it, every entry appears in the cheat-sheet (no orphan handler, no secret shortcut); Open destinations come from the REAL router (`lib/navDestinations.ts`), Launch entries from the REAL `/actions` (DO-NOT #11 — no hardcoded slash-command/phase, incl. palette labels; AC9). **Windows-first, both chords (AC3, `lib/formatChord.ts` the ONLY renderer):** platform detection DEFAULTS to `Ctrl` when unknown — a Mac-only `⌘K` hint anywhere fails; the cheat-sheet prints BOTH a Windows column and a Mac column. **Keyboard map (AC4):** `Ctrl/⌘+K` palette, `?` cheat-sheet, `j`/`k` a VISIBLE list selection on board/triage/projects/inbox (one `useListKeyboardNav` scoped to the content region; rows opt in with `data-nav-item`), Enter opens (the item's own handler — real DOM `Tab` order intact). **Density (AC5, `hooks/useDensity.ts`, persisted via `useLocalStorage`):** comfortable⇄compact on board/projects/triage, token-driven off `data-density` (no magic numbers), a header `DensityToggle` on each + a palette command. **Terminal focus mode (AC5):** `t` focuses the terminal + enters focus mode, Esc leaves — through the EXISTING A18 maximize toggle (the collapse→resize path that fires the pty resize), NO parallel hide path. **Optimistic UI (AC6):** the query surfaces already render TanStack-cached data on the first frame (spinner gated on cold-fetch, not `isFetching`); optimistic updates stay confined to WebUI-owned state (filters/density/boardColumn/dismissals) and NEVER claim a Claude-session outcome the WebUI cannot observe (`liveSession` is pty-existence). **a11y (AC7):** the palette traps focus, is labelled + screen-reader operable, a visible focus ring everywhere; every keyed action has a clickable equivalent. Every new/changed file ≤300 LOC; `client/src/lib/externalApi.ts` NOT grown; bloat baseline not ratcheted; `doc-sync.test.ts` green. Visual baselines: `board` · `projects` · `triage` · `task-detail` (focus-mode affordance) + the new `palette-open` (orchestrator regenerates the pinned-container PNGs).

01-adopted::FR-01.66.title: The Mission tab is a LIVE view of what a session is doing, driven by the raw `~/.claude/**/<uuid>.jsonl` transcript — not only a finished-run record (iterate `iterate-2026-07-17-mission-live-jsonl`, standalone post-campaign feature approved by Sven 2026-07-17; change_type=feature). **THE BEHAVIOURAL REQUIREMENT (load-bearing — AC1/AC3):** for ANY session — a live iterate/run OR an ad-hoc session with an actively-written JSONL but NO `work_completed` run row — the Mission tab renders a business-language summary, an inferred lifecycle stage (or an honest `—` when it cannot be derived), and a plain-language, rolling narration of what the JSONL actually contains; it NO LONGER shows a permanent "No run data yet — nothing to prove" for such sessions. **THE THREE PANELS (Sven's design):** the **LEFT** panel replaces the Requirement/Spec/Test/Review/Commit audit RAIL with ONE coherent panel everywhere — (1) a short business-language summary ("what this is", Ship's-Log register, no jargon), (2) **where it stands now** shown with the four stage labels **`Spec` · `Build` · `Test` · `Finalize`** (verbatim, Sven's call — NOT gerunds; a test pins the four strings, AC4), and (3) the audit trail folded into clickable **artifact links** (spec · diff · test results · ADR · commit) that open in the RIGHT panel — for a COMPLETED run the finished summary + a `Finalize`-done state + the Req/Spec/Test/Review/Commit content preserved AS those links, no audit information lost (AC2); the **MIDDLE** (Operation) card becomes a plain-language live summary of the JSONL (the A10 narrator pointed at the raw transcript via a new deterministic `narrator-transcript.ts` summarizer — a rolling "what's happening now" line + a short recent-activity list); the **RIGHT** (Artifact) panel is unchanged — artifacts open here on click. **DATA FLOW (reuse, no new poll):** the middle consumes the SAME `useTaskTranscript` poll TaskDetailPage already runs (no second poller, no new server surface); a new `useMissionLive` view-model composes the transcript summary + the existing `useRunDetail` join + `useMissionState` (`state==="active"`=live, NOT `liveSession`); completed-run artifacts + summary come from the existing `/runs/:runId` join; artifacts open in the existing `ArtifactPanel` via the existing file/tree routes. **HONESTY FENCES (non-negotiable — AC3/AC5):** the narration reflects ONLY what the JSONL actually contains — no fabricated liveness (no run data AND no transcript → an honest "waiting", never invented activity), the stage shows ONLY when derivable else `—`, no count/number the JSONL did not produce; JSONL is an untrusted io-boundary so every narrated string is control/bidi-stripped + length-capped; read-only observer throughout (rules 1/12, DO-NOT #1) — never writes `~/.claude/projects/**`, `shipwright_run_config.json` or `run_loop_state.json`; `liveSession` is never rendered as "Claude is listening/running". **THE TERMINAL FENCE:** `client/src/components/terminal/**` + `server/src/terminal/**` have ZERO diff (the transcript is read via the existing `useTaskTranscript` hook, not a terminal-component change). **Reduced-motion (A20):** every panel renders its COMPLETE final state under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`; any roll-in of the narration is enhancement only. Scope: the campaign-orchestrator special-case (show campaign progress as the stage) is a FAST FOLLOW, NOT this iterate. Every new/changed file ≤300 LOC; `client/src/lib/externalApi.ts` NOT grown; bloat baseline not ratcheted; `doc-sync.test.ts` green. Visual baselines: `task-detail-mission` + `task-detail-mission-live` move (the left/middle panels change; orchestrator regenerates the pinned-container PNGs).

01-adopted::FR-01.67.title: The Mission "Where it stands" lifecycle gains its two missing bookends and becomes correct for a long autonomous-campaign session (iterate `iterate-2026-07-17-mission-stages-campaign`, standalone fast-follow to FR-01.66 approved by Sven 2026-07-17; change_type=feature; extends FR-01.66). **SIX STAGES (AC1):** `STAGE_LABELS` becomes EXACTLY **`Analyze` · `Spec` · `Build` · `Test` · `Finalize` · `Merge`** (verbatim, Sven's call — a test pins the six strings). `narrator-transcript.ts inferStage()` gains **Analyze** (NEW first — the leading `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob`/`Task`/`TodoWrite` scout cluster before the first edit, or a `/shipwright-iterate`/`analyze`/`scout` kickoff incl. `--campaign … --autonomous`; the WEAKEST signal, overridden by any real edit/test) and **Merge** (NEW last — SPLIT out of Finalize: `git push`, `\bgh pr\b`, `gh pr merge`, `gh run`, and the first-class `pr-link` event, the strongest "pushed, awaiting the squash" marker); **Finalize NARROWED** to `git commit` + `changelog`/`decision_log` edits and NO LONGER swallows push/PR; a completed (merged) run → `Merge`, `stageComplete=true` (terminal); honesty preserved — `null`→`—`, never guessed. **THE LOAD-BEARING FIX — windowed stage (AC2):** `inferStage` OR-latches across the whole transcript and never resets, which is WRONG for a campaign (many serial sub-iterates in ONE session log) — once sub-iterate #1 opens a PR, Merge latches for the multi-hour session while sub-iterate #21 is still in Build. A new `currentIterateEvents(events)` helper slices to ONLY the current (sub-)iterate — the LATEST boundary being its `/shipwright-iterate` kickoff (inclusive) or the event after a prior sub-iterate's terminal `pr-link` (clamped so a trailing pr-link stays its own Merge, never an empty window) — and `inferStage` runs on that slice; a plain single iterate is unchanged (the boundary is the session start). (`work_completed` is NOT a boundary here — it lives in `shipwright_events.jsonl`, not the Claude `<uuid>.jsonl` the narrator reads.) **AUTONOMOUS-CAMPAIGN AWARENESS (AC3, no new server surface):** the orchestrator task's title is `campaign: <slug>` (the sole durable webui breadcrumb; `useLaunchCampaign`); a tiny `parseCampaignSlug(title)` recovers the slug (prefix-anchored + case-sensitive on the exact producer breadcrumb — a human "Campaign: Q3 planning" never parses); given a slug, `useMissionLive` enables the EXISTING `useCampaigns(projectId)` 3 s poll (DORMANT `{enabled:false}` for every normal session — NOT a second transcript poller) and `deriveCampaignInfo` reads `done`/`total` + the active sub-iterate (`steps.find(in_progress)?.id ?? nextPending?.id`); `MissionLeftPanel` shows a progress line **"Sub-iterate N of M · A<k>"** above the stepper and the `businessSummary` becomes the human-readable slug, not the raw `campaign: <slug>` title. `campaign?:{slug,done,total,activeSubIterate}
